title: Power BI Slicers, Sync, Bookmarks, Field Parameters, and Cross-Filter Control

confirmed_facts.fact: Slicer styles: List (vertical/horizontal), Dropdown (compact), Between (date range slider — requires Date-type column), Relative Date (rolling window — Last N days/months/years, requires Date-type column), Tile/Button (small fixed set).

Sync slicers: View > Sync slicers panel. Toggle Sync (selection propagates) and Visible (control shown) per page. Slicers only sync if they use the same table[column]. Assign group name for related slicers.

Bookmarks capture: visual visibility, slicer selections, filter states. Used for Monthly/Quarterly/Yearly view switching with buttons. Scope bookmarks to specific visuals to avoid resetting unrelated filters.

Field parameters (May 2022+) let users dynamically swap axis fields via a slicer. One visual can show 'by Month', 'by Quarter', 'by Year' without bookmarks. DAX: Period Grouping = {("Month", NAMEOF(DateTable[MonthYear]), 0), ...}.

Cross-filter: by default, clicking a visual cross-filters all others on the page. Control via Format > Edit Interactions: Filter (remove non-matching), Highlight (dim non-matching), None. Slicers always filter — no highlight/none option.

implementation_notes:
Default last 12 months — four methods:

1. Relative date slicer (recommended): DateTable[Date] > Style = Relative date > Last 12 Months. Auto-adjusts.

2. Bookmark default: Create bookmark with slicer at 'last 12 months', right-click > Set as default.

3. Visual-level filter: DateTable[MonthSort] > Top N, 12, by MonthSort DESC. Always shows latest 12.

4. Report-level filter: DateTable[Date] relative 'last 12 months'. Users cannot override unless visible.

Sync slicers setup:
1. Select slicer on source page
2. View > Sync slicers panel
3. Per page: toggle Sync=ON (responds to selection), Visible=ON/OFF (shows slicer UI)
4. Page with Sync=ON, Visible=OFF: filters correctly but no visible slicer control
5. All synced slicers must use the same field (same table[column])

Reset button: create bookmark with slicer at default state → add Button visual → Action > Bookmark

Bookmark-based view switching (Monthly/Quarterly/Yearly):
1. Build 3 matrix visuals stacked on same position:
   - Matrix A: Columns = DateTable[MonthYear]
   - Matrix B: Columns = DateTable[QuarterLabel]
   - Matrix C: Columns = DateTable[YearStr]
2. Selection pane: show Matrix A, hide B+C → create bookmark 'Monthly View'
3. Show B, hide A+C → bookmark 'Quarterly View'
4. Show C, hide A+B → bookmark 'Yearly View'
5. Add 3 buttons with Action > Bookmark pointing to each

Scope bookmarks to specific visuals (Edit > uncheck 'All visuals') to prevent resetting unrelated slicers.

Field parameters (cleaner alternative to bookmarks):
```dax
// Auto-generated DAX when creating field parameter
Period Grouping = {
    ("Month",   NAMEOF('DateTable'[MonthYear]),    0),
    ("Quarter", NAMEOF('DateTable'[QuarterLabel]), 1),
    ("Year",    NAMEOF('DateTable'[YearStr]),      2)
}
```

Setup: Modeling > New parameter > Fields > add the 3 date columns > Create.
Use: drag [Period Grouping] to chart Axis instead of a specific column.
Slicer auto-generated for switching.

Also works for metric switching: parameter of measures ([Sev5 Count], [Sev4 Count], etc.) with slicer.

Cross-filter control:
1. Select source visual
2. Format ribbon > Edit Interactions
3. Each other visual shows icons: Filter (funnel) / Highlight / None
4. Click icon on target to set interaction
5. Format > Edit Interactions again to exit

For security dashboards: Filter is usually clearer than Highlight.
To prevent a slicer from filtering a specific 'reference' visual: select slicer > Edit Interactions > set target to None.
caveats: Relative date slicer requires Date-type column — text columns like DateTable[MonthYear] don't work
Syncing slicers using different columns (one uses [MonthYear], another uses [Date]) fails silently — standardise to same column
Bookmarks capturing all visuals reset unrelated slicers on click — scope to specific visuals
Field parameters sort key comes from underlying column Sort by Column setting — not the parameter table
New report pages start unsynchronised — remember to add them to Sync slicers group
Report-level relative date filter prevents analysts from selecting custom ranges — use page-level or slicer instead
Date range slider (Between style) requires numeric or Date type — doesn't work with text MonthYear column
